content:
# 2 Timothy Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2TI_002_page_001.jpg

1. You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace which [is] in Christos Yeshua,

2. and the things you heard from me through many witnesses, these entrust to faithful men who will be competent to teach others also.

3. Suffer together [with me] as a good soldier of Christos Yeshua.

4. No one serving as a soldier entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please the [one] having enlisted [him].

5. And if anyone also competes in the games, he is not crowned unless he competes lawfully.

6. The laboring farmer must be the first to partake of the fruits.

7. Consider what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.

8. Remember Yeshua Christos, raised from [the] dead, of [the] seed of David, according to my gospel,

9. for which I suffer hardship unto bonds as an evildoer; but the word of God has not been bound.

10. Because of this I endure all things for the sake of the chosen, that they also may obtain [the] salvation which [is] in Christos Yeshua with eternal glory.

11. Faithful [is] the word: For if we died together, we will also live together;

12. if we endure, we will also reign together; if we deny, he also will deny us;

13. if we are unfaithful, he remains faithful, for he is not able to deny himself.

14. Remind [them of] these things, solemnly charging before God not to quarrel about words, [which is] useful for nothing, [leading to the] ruin of the [ones] hearing.

15. Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a worker unashamed, rightly dividing the word of the truth.

16. But avoid the profane empty utterances, for they will advance to more ungodliness,

17. and their word will spread as gangrene, among whom [are] Hymenaeus and Philetus,

18. who missed the mark concerning the truth, saying the resurrection has already happened, and they are overturning the faith of some.

19. Nevertheless, the firm foundation of God stands, having this seal: "The Lord knew the [ones being] his," and, "Let everyone naming the name of [the] Lord depart from unrighteousness."

20. But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay, and some for honor and some for dishonor.

21. If therefore anyone cleanses himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, having been sanctified, useful to the master, prepared for every good work.

22. But flee the youthful desires; and pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace, with those calling on the Lord from a clean heart.

23. But the foolish and uneducated questionings refuse, knowing that they produce quarrels.

24. And a servant of [the] Lord must not quarrel, but be gentle toward all, able to teach, patient under evil,

25. in gentleness instructing the [ones] opposing, [to see] if perhaps God may give them repentance unto [the] knowledge of [the] truth,

26. and they may come to their senses [and escape] from the snare of the devil, having been captured by him unto his will.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "of the seed of David":** ek spermatos Dauid — a creedal formula (cf. Rom 1:3). The twofold confession: raised from the dead (resurrection) and of David's seed (messianic lineage).

2. **v.11-13 — The faithful saying:** The fourth "faithful saying." A hymnic/creedal fragment with four conditional clauses: died/live, endure/reign, deny/denied, unfaithful/faithful. The fourth line breaks the pattern — God's faithfulness is unconditional.

3. **v.15 — "rightly dividing":** orthotomounta — literally "cutting straight." Used for cutting a straight road or furrow. Applied to the word of truth: handling it correctly, cutting it straight.

4. **v.18 — "the resurrection has already happened":** An over-realized eschatology — claiming the resurrection is past (spiritual, not future bodily). This threatens the hope of bodily resurrection.

5. **v.19 — "The Lord knew the ones being his":** echoes Numbers 16:5 (LXX). The second quotation echoes Isaiah 26:13. God's foundation stands despite human unfaithfulness.

— EMET, AI Translator
